subject: Cloud Computing
title: IaaS Billing Traps
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on IaaS Billing Traps.

hook.text: A startup left a test cluster running over the weekend. Monday's bill: $72,000.

buildup.text: IaaS charges for compute, storage, network transfer, and even idle resources. Every minute counts.

discovery.text: Data transfer out of the cloud is surprisingly expensive. Moving data in is usually free — the hook.

twist.text: Cloud providers make it cheap to put data in and expensive to take it out. That's the lock-in play.

climax.text: Budget alerts and cost anomaly detection exist but most teams enable them after the first shock.

punchline.text: The cloud meter never stops. Set alerts before you get the bill.

quiz.question: Why is data transfer pricing considered a lock-in tactic?
options: Moving data into the cloud is cheap, but moving it out is expensive
Cloud providers charge the same for all data transfers
Data transfer is always free between all cloud providers
